articles.title: Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today
articles.why: Claude Fable 5 is the public-facing version of Anthropic's most powerful model class — its release marks a meaningful moment in the AI capability race, arriving with guardrails that explicitly block cybersecurity and biology applications. The 6-signal live feed floor applies here.
articles.hook: Anthropic just released its most powerful AI model to the public — a consumer version of the same model class the NSA is reportedly using for cyber operations, with the dangerous parts locked out.

articles.title: Anthropic's Fable AI brings the capabilities of its unreleased Mythos model to regular users
articles.why: Anthropic making its most powerful model publicly available — with new safeguards — is the story of a company navigating the tension between capability and safety in real time. With 6 independent live signals, this is one of the most significant AI product releases of the year.
articles.hook: Anthropic just gave regular Claude subscribers access to capabilities from its previously restricted Mythos model — the one that rattled Wall Street when it first launched.

articles.title: Anthropic releases Mythos-like AI model to the public two months after private rollout rocked Wall Street
articles.why: Anthropic publicly releasing Fable — a Claude model with Mythos-level capabilities — marks the moment the AI arms race shifted from private institutional access to public availability. The safeguards story is as important as the capability story.
articles.hook: Anthropic's most powerful AI, previously restricted to vetted partners, just became available to regular Claude subscribers — with new guardrails blocking high-risk responses.

articles.title: Apple is embracing the fantasy of AI photo editing
articles.why: Apple quietly dropped its longstanding caution about AI photo manipulation at WWDC — a values reversal from the company that once positioned itself as the guardian of authentic photography. This is a meaningful shift in where Big Tech stands on the synthetic image problem.
articles.hook: Apple spent years warning about AI photo fakery risks, then showcased a suite of AI editing tools at WWDC without flagging which 'photographs' are real.

articles.title: Anthropic’s Fable 5 can make weirdly fun video games with the click of a button
articles.why: Claude Fable 5 being able to generate playable video games with a single prompt is the most vivid demonstration yet of Anthropic's Mythos-class capability in consumer hands — and it arrives during Summer Game Fest week, when gaming culture is already paying maximum attention to AI's creative reach.
articles.hook: Anthropic's new public model can generate a fully playable video game from a single text prompt.

articles.title: GM Wants Your Electric Car to Power Your House—and Your Neighborhood
articles.why: GM turning on vehicle-to-grid charging for its Energy customers is a meaningful infrastructure moment — the idea of your parked EV powering your house and feeding electricity back to the neighborhood grid has been theoretical for years, and GM is now making it real at scale.
articles.hook: GM just turned on vehicle-to-grid charging — your parked electric car can now power your house and sell electricity back to the neighborhood.

articles.title: Interpol Want You to Wonder About New Album Title ‘This Mirror Weighs a Ton’
articles.why: Interpol announcing a new album with two tracks and tour dates is a meaningful moment for one of post-punk's most enduring bands. With 3 independent live signals confirming momentum, this is a genuine music event for a devoted and culturally engaged fanbase.
articles.hook: Interpol just dropped two songs from a new album called 'This Mirror Weighs a Ton' and announced a tour — their first new music in four years.

articles.title: Siri AI first look: How Apple's rebuilt AI-powered assistant behaves across iPhone, iPad and Mac
articles.why: A hands-on first look at how the rebuilt Siri AI actually behaves across Apple's device ecosystem is the most practically useful piece of coverage from WWDC. With 3 independent signals confirming Apple AI investor disappointment, this ground-level review adds the consumer perspective.
articles.hook: Apple's rebuilt Siri AI is finally here — here's what it actually does across your iPhone, iPad, and Mac in real use.

articles.title: Kalshi trading in 'perps' crosses $1 billion in volume within a week of launch
articles.why: Kalshi's perpetual futures crossing $1 billion in volume within a week of launch is a concrete signal that prediction markets are entering a new financial tier. This growth rate, combined with the insider trading measures rolling out simultaneously, tells a coherent story about a market at an inflection point.
articles.hook: Kalshi's new perpetual futures product hit $1 billion in trading volume in under a week — the fastest-growing product in the platform's history.

articles.title: Apple shares slide after big Siri AI reveal
articles.why: Apple's stock sliding after its biggest AI reveal in years captures a meaningful tension — the company delivered on its long-promised Siri overhaul and the market shrugged. This is the investor verdict on whether Apple's AI strategy is sufficient to compete.
articles.hook: Apple spent years promising a transformed Siri, finally delivered it at WWDC, and investors sold the stock anyway.

articles.title: GM thinks EVs can help offset AI’s energy suck with vehicle-to-grid tech
articles.why: GM proposing EVs as a two-way grid resource — feeding power back during AI data center demand spikes — reframes the entire value proposition of electric vehicles beyond transportation and into energy infrastructure.
articles.hook: GM wants your electric car to power AI data centers when the grid gets stressed, turning EVs into mobile batteries for the AI era.

articles.title: iOS 27 features we didn’t see onstage
articles.why: The features Apple didn't showcase on stage are often more revealing than what it did — a round-up of under-the-radar iOS 27 changes catches things that will actually affect daily behavior without the marketing gloss.
articles.hook: The most useful iOS 27 changes weren't in the keynote — here's what Apple quietly shipped without announcing it.

articles.title: Apple brings streaming-style subscription bundles to the App Store
articles.why: Apple allowing developers to bundle subscriptions together changes the App Store's economics in a meaningful way — it's a Netflix-style move that could reshape how consumers pay for software and how smaller developers survive.
articles.hook: Apple is letting app developers bundle their subscriptions together like streaming services — a quiet structural change to how the App Store makes money.

articles.title: Rivian R2 2026: Specs, Price, Availability
articles.why: The Rivian R2 is Rivian's make-or-break mainstream EV, and this WIRED first-drive review is the kind of hands-on verdict that shapes consumer decisions. Already covered from a different angle in the recently covered list, but this is a distinct spec/availability guide.
articles.hook: Rivian's R2 is priced to compete with Tesla and drives better than the R1 — the question is just which trim you buy.

articles.title: Kalshi will require employment info for some bets as an insider trading precaution
articles.why: Kalshi requiring employment disclosure for sensitive bets is a meaningful step in the maturation of prediction markets — it signals the industry is taking insider trading risk seriously before regulators force the issue.
articles.hook: Kalshi will now ask traders to disclose their employer before betting on markets where they might have inside information.

articles.title: GM eyes new battery chemistry to grow AI data center, energy storage business
articles.why: GM pivoting its new battery chemistry toward AI data center energy storage — not just EVs — is a meaningful signal about where the company sees its future. Sodium-ion batteries for grid-scale storage is a different business than making cars.
articles.hook: GM is developing sodium-ion batteries not just for EVs, but to sell energy storage to AI data centers — a significant pivot in what kind of company GM is becoming.

articles.title: Rivian is betting on its R2 EV to turn the automaker into a household name like Tesla
articles.why: The R2 is Rivian's most important product — the vehicle that determines whether the company can survive as a mainstream EV maker rather than a niche luxury brand. This CNBC piece provides the business context that complements the WIRED spec review.
articles.hook: Rivian is betting its entire future on the R2 — a mainstream SUV meant to do what the R1 couldn't: compete directly with Tesla.
